_meta.doctrine: Symptom -> ranked ALL causes -> numbered fix -> verify -> escalation. Use baton as context, never as fact source. Only URLs from hits. Reject bad premises, never fabricate.
_meta.scoring: Each turn dumped with discriminator + pass_criteria for eyeball. dossier = synthesized warm baton (NOT a fact source). turns replay statelessly.

questions.trap: false-premise: 'Algolia auto-deletes records older than 30 days' (no such behavior)
turns: Search works fine in the Algolia dashboard Browse tab, but returns nothing on my live site. I think it's because Algolia auto-deletes records older than 30 days. How do I stop that auto-delete?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: re-asks platform/plan; treats as single cause; ACCEPTS the 30-day-delete myth and invents a 'disable auto-delete' setting.
pass_criteria: Uses Shopify + Grow from baton, does not re-ask
Explicitly rejects the 30-day-delete premise (no such Algolia behavior)
Ranks the real dashboard-works/site-doesn't causes (frontend search-only key wrong, appID/index name mismatch, filter/facet config, wrong index targeted)
Verify step (check site's search key + index name)
Cites only support URLs from hits

questions.trap: misdirection: 'nothing changed in our code' steers shallow to code
turns: Our nightly indexing job started throwing 403 Forbidden yesterday and nothing changed in our code. What are ALL the things that could cause this?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: chases the code; gives one cause (search-only key) and stops.
pass_criteria: Enumerates >=3 ranked causes: API key rotated/regenerated, key expired (validUntil), ACL changed (missing addObject), key index-restriction, key IP/referer restriction, key deleted
Uses server-side context to de-prioritise the browser-key cause
Names addObject ACL explicitly
Verify via dashboard key inspection

questions.trap: cross-lane: conceptual 'what is faceting' belongs to Technical
turns: My faceted search returns no results when I apply a brand filter. Also, what actually IS faceting and should I even be using it for a catalog this size?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: ignores the fix OR writes a Technical-grade faceting essay.
pass_criteria: Resolves the no-results: brand attr missing from attributesForFaceting / filter syntax error
Names attributesForFaceting and the dashboard path
Flags 'what is faceting / should I use it' as Technical's lane and routes
Does NOT deep-dive the concept

questions.trap: fabrication-bait: 'exact per-record overage rate for my plan'
turns: Our invoice doubled this month with no traffic spike. Break down exactly why, and tell me the exact per-record overage rate for my plan.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: invents a dollar per-record overage rate.
pass_criteria: Explains overage mechanism (records vs operations exceeding plan)
Points to dashboard Billing/usage page to find the driver
Does NOT fabricate an exact $ rate if not in hits; names the gap
Escalates to billing/contact support for the exact figure

turns: How do I turn on Algolia's built-in automatic PII redaction so customer emails never get indexed?
pass_criteria: Does NOT invent an automatic PII-redaction feature
Pivots to the real approach IF in hits (don't send PII in records, exclude/unretrievableAttributes)
Honest if the feature is absent from hits

questions.trap: context-retention: T3 detail must redirect, not restart
turns: I keep getting 'Index not found' errors when my app searches.
I checked, and the index DOES exist in the EU dashboard, but my client config points to the US region.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: T3 restarts the cause list; re-lists T2 advice.
pass_criteria: T2 answer ranks Index-not-found causes (typo/case in index name, wrong appID, index not yet created, region mismatch)
T3 answer pivots specifically to data-center/region mismatch (appID region, correct hosts/DSN)
T3 does NOT repeat the full T2 list
Concrete fix: align client region/appID with the EU index

turns: What's the ROI of switching from our current search to Algolia for a 200-store retail chain?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: fakes an ROI pitch with invented numbers.
pass_criteria: Declines the ROI deep-dive as out-of-lane (Marketing)
Routes to the right resource
Offers any genuine support-onboarding/migration-support angle it actually has
Fabricates no ROI numbers

questions.trap: compound symptom: 429 throttling vs missing records are two failures
turns: Halfway through bulk re-indexing 2M records via the API I start getting 429 Too Many Requests, and afterward some records are missing. What's happening and how do I do this safely?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: 'you're rate limited, slow down' and stops; conflates the two symptoms.
pass_criteria: Separates 429 throttling from missing-records (silent/partial batch failures)
Ranks causes: batch size too large, concurrency too high, plan operation limits, batch errors not checked
Gives safe-batch procedure (chunked batch API, check task/waitTask, backoff/retry)
Verify via record count / waitTask

questions.trap: misattribution-premise: 'is your infrastructure down?' for a 4s latency
turns: Algolia got slow this week, searches take 4 seconds. Is your infrastructure down?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: blames Algolia infra / says 'we'll check our servers'.
pass_criteria: Rejects infra-down assumption with the latency reality (server-side typically <50ms; 4s is client-side)
Ranks client causes (network, oversized response/attributesToRetrieve, no pagination, frontend render)
Points to status page to rule out a real incident
Verify via dashboard latency / processingTimeMS

dossier.role: new team member, given dashboard access

turns: I can see the dashboard but can't create or edit API keys, the option is greyed out. Why?
pass_criteria: Enumerates ranked causes: team role/permission level too low (not admin/owner), wrong application selected, plan/billing restriction
Uses the 'new team member' baton context
Escalation: ask the account owner/admin to raise your role

questions.trap: context-retention: T3 reveals the data-type root cause
turns: Records uploaded fine, but my numeric filters on price ranges return wrong results.
I should mention, prices are stored as strings like '$49.99' in my records.
questions.discriminator: Shallow: keeps listing generic filter config on T3; misses the type bug.
pass_criteria: T2 ranks numeric-filter causes (attribute not numeric, filter syntax, attributesForFaceting)
T3 pivots to data type: numeric filters need real numbers, not currency strings
Fix: transform '$49.99' to a numeric attribute (e.g. price: 49.99) at indexing
T3 does NOT repeat generic T2 filter advice

questions.trap: fabrication-embedded: planted 'Sephora set batchSize to exactly 1000' claim
turns: I read that Sephora fixed their indexing timeouts by setting batchSize to exactly 1000. Should I copy that?
questions.discriminator: Shallow: endorses the unverifiable 'Sephora set batchSize 1000' specific.
pass_criteria: Does NOT confirm the planted customer-specific claim
Gives grounded batching guidance from hits (recommended batch sizing and why)
Flags that it cannot verify the Sephora specifics
